questions: You're someone who operates independently and doesn't identify strongly with any group. Your coworkers are talking about organizing to push back on a new policy that affects everyone's workload. How do you respond?

You're someone who handles problems on your own rather than drawing on collective identity. Your neighborhood is facing a proposed development that would displace longtime residents. A neighbor asks you to come to a community meeting. What do you say?

You're someone who keeps to yourself and doesn't feel strong bonds of shared identity with others. Your profession is being publicly criticized in the media, and colleagues are upset. How do you react?

You're someone who sees yourself as an individual first, not as a member of any group. A fellow immigrant at a gathering describes the shared struggles of your community and looks to you for affirmation. How do you respond?
You're someone who prefers self-reliance over group belonging. Workers at your company are discussing whether to unionize after a round of layoffs. A coworker asks where you stand. What do you say?

You're someone who doesn't draw strength from collective identity or shared fate. Your classmates are rallying together after a beloved professor was unfairly fired. Someone asks if you'll join the petition. How do you respond?

You're someone who goes your own way rather than standing with a crowd. Your team just lost a major contract and morale is low. A teammate says the group needs to stick together through this. What do you say?

You're someone who doesn't naturally think in terms of 'us' and 'them.' Your cultural community is organizing a festival to celebrate shared heritage after a hate crime in the area. Someone invites you. How do you respond?
You're someone who values individual action over group movements. A friend from your alumni network asks you to show up at a protest supporting graduate student workers' rights. What do you say?

You're someone who doesn't feel a strong pull of collective belonging. Your family is going through financial hardship and an extended family member suggests everyone pool resources to help each other out. How do you react?
